task: Advanced high-economic-value task: Reality Capture Progress Deviation Quantification. Context: a program manager is choosing between hold, release, retest, redesign, or targeted mitigation using only supplied evidence for reality capture progress deviation quantification in the construction technology domain. Embedded packet: records: R1 planned_pct=31.92 lidar_pct=33.47 payapp_pct=76.56 overbill_amount=69576; R2 planned_pct=33.2 lidar_pct=23.93 payapp_pct=40.47 overbill_amount=39268; R3 planned_pct=59.28 lidar_pct=49.89 payapp_pct=51.83 overbill_amount=55271; R4 planned_pct=59.46 lidar_pct=37.21 payapp_pct=45.81 overbill_amount=68929; normalized_threshold_score=52.5; remediation_budget_usd=130000; options: O1 targets the planned_pct driver and reduces normalized risk contribution by 12% cost=40300, O2 targets the lidar_pct driver and reduces normalized risk contribution by 18% cost=61100, O3 targets the overbill_amount margin driver and improves normalized margin by 9% cost=70200; source_excerpt="review must separate confirmed evidence from unresolved compliance gaps". Rules and constraints: score = first metric + 0.6*second metric - 0.25*third metric after unit normalization; score above normalized_threshold_score is fail and within 10% below the limit is warn; remediation options may be combined only if total cost<=remediation_budget_usd; expert-review flag is required when any record changes classification after a 5% sensitivity shift; all calculations must use only the embedded packet. Required work: rebuild the decision logic, identify threshold crossings, test the sensitivity of the conclusion, and create the review-ready table. Deliver progress_deviation.csv + superintendent_brief.md with formulas or executable logic, intermediate calculations, row-level citations to the embedded packet, explicit assumptions, validation checks, sensitivity notes where relevant, and a final decision table. Do not invent inputs, substitute outside data, or hide uncertainty; mark any unresolved ambiguity as needs_review.
